prestazioni: Mercedes-AMG F1 M09 EQ Power+ 1.6L V6 turbo hybrid (engine code: M09 E Performance) with a custom Bosch Motorsport MGU-K (Motor Generator Unit-Kinetic) upgrade to 120 kW (161 hp) output, replacing the stock 120 kW unit for increased energy recovery under braking and deployment during acceleration phases

Pankl Racing Systems titanium connecting rods with ARP Custom Age 625+ fasteners, paired with Mahle Motorsport forged pistons (9.3:1 compression ratio) to handle the increased boost pressure from a Garrett Motorsports F1-spec turbocharger (GTX5544R Gen II with ball-bearing CHRA) pushing 3.5 bar absolute pressure, controlled via a MoTeC M150 ECU with Williams-developed torque shaping maps

Brembo Racing CCM-R carbon-ceramic brake discs (32mm front, 28mm rear) with AP Racing CP9436-2 calipers (6-piston front, 4-piston rear) and Ferodo Racing DS3000 brake pads, paired with a Williams-designed brake-by-wire system recalibrated for 70% front bias under heavy braking (adjustable via steering wheel dial)
estetica: Williams Advanced Engineering carbon-fiber front wing with a 2022-spec FIA-compliant Y250 vortex generator and adjustable Gurney flaps (3-position, +5mm increments) for high-speed stability, paired with a rear wing featuring a double-DRS system (primary and secondary actuators) for drag reduction on straights

RML Group titanium exhaust system with Inconel 625 headers and a 4-into-2-into-1 collector, exiting through a single central outlet with a Akrapović titanium muffler (F1-spec noise compliance, 105 dB limit)

Custom livery by JLF Designs featuring a matte anthracite base with iridescent blue-to-purple shift (inspired by Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team’s 2021 W12) and Williams Racing heritage logos in 3D-printed titanium (SLM Solutions process) for weight reduction and durability
